about

about

our vision

At retrofit construction we recognize the impact construction and buildings have on our environments and we want to be part of the solution and not the problem. While the construction industry in general is getting better at building more sustainable buildings we strive to improve the buildings of the past. Craftsmanship was very prominent in the past but energy efficiency wasn't for lack of interest and understanding of building sciences. We believe that the most sustainable building is the one that is already built and we help our clients make their buildings more sustainable, durable, comfortable and healthy. After all we spend most of our time indoors and as the great Winston Churchill said: "We shape our buildings; thereafter they shape us."

our offer

Retrofit Construction is a general contractor and consulting firm specialized in deep energy retrofits. We are committed to creating and building sustainable, durable, comfortable, and healthy environments. We give existing commercial, and residential buildings a new life through well-planned energy efficient upgrades. We are located in Montreal, Quebec and service the greater Montreal area for our construction mandates. Our team of experts is experienced in conducting comprehensive energy audits, analyzing the building envelope, and recommending the most effective solutions to optimize energy performance. From building envelope upgrades such as added insulation and air sealing to HVAC and lighting upgrades, we offer a wide range of retrofitting services tailored to each client's needs. Our goal is to help our clients save energy, create a more comfortable and healthy environment, and contribute to a more sustainable future.

our founder

Fellipe Falluh, a builder and consultant in deep energy retrofits, has dedicated his career to promoting sustainability in the built environment. With an engineering degree from McGill University, Fellipe proudly founded his business venture at just 24 years old; a company called Retrofit Construction, which he plans to expand across Quebec. At the conference, he will share his insights on his recently completed Deep Energy Retrofit of a duplex in Montreal, which is on its way to become the first Net-Zero Ready Certified building in Quebec.
